The Tanzania Commission for Universities (TCU) through its mandate
as spelt out in the Universities Act, Cap. 346 of the Laws of Tanzania
coordinates admissions of Bachelor students in Higher Education
Institutions (HEIs) in the country. To facilitate the process, TCU
prepares Admission Guidebooks annually to guide the prospective
applicants on admission procedures, available programmes, and
institutions approved to admit applicants in a particular academic year.
to provide the applicants with basic
information related to the programmes approved by TCU or NACTVET
to admit applicants in the 2025/2026 admission cycle, their institutions,
locations, programmes entry requirements, capacities, and durations.
This Guidebook also provides fresh insight to those already associated
with higher education within and outside Tanzania.
As a matter of procedures, applications for admissions are submitted
directly to respective institutions through online admission systems
obtained from institutions’ websites. Applicants are strongly advised to
carefully read and understand procedures and entry requirements
before lodging their applications. The selection process is based on the
minimum programme entry qualifications and other requirements of
each institution, including competitions and admission capacity. The
decision on admission of applicants is made by the individual
institutions through their Senates or Governing Boards. For further
inquiries, prospective applicants and the general public are advised to
contact respective institutions through the contact addresses provided

1.3.1 Entry schemes and pathways into Bachelor’s degree
Two major entry schemes to Bachelor’s degree programmes are recognised
i) The direct entry qualifications, with the following pathways:
a) Holders of Form Six Qualifications
b) Holders of Foreign Secondary Education Certificates;
ii) The equivalent entry qualifications, with the following pathways:
a) Holders of Ordinary Diploma or Equivalent Qualifications
b) Holders of Foundation Certificate granted by the Open
c) Holders of a Bachelor’s degree award.

TCU is required under the provisions of section 5 (1) (b) of The Universities
Act, Cap. 346 of the Laws of Tanzania to audit, on a regular basis, the quality
assurance mechanisms of universities. The audit process includes among
others; carrying out evaluation, assessment and monitoring of university
institutions on a continuous basis, preparing and revising guidelines for
quality assurance and other related matters, for the purposes of monitoring
and regulating general management and performance of universities.
To facilitate this process, the Commission has been dully mandated under
the provisions of section 16 of The Universities Act, Cap. 346 of the Laws of
Tanzania, as amended, to charge and collect quality assurance fees. In view
of this mandate, the Commission at its 64
approved Tshs. 20,000/= being quality assurance fee which has to be paid
by every student enrolled to a university institution regardless of the
programme of study. The legal provisions thus obligate all university
institutions to collect and remit annually to the Commission the prescribed
quality assurance fee for every student
